_note: The Side-Quest Catalog. Each quest runs on the QuestManager engine (quests.js / C# QuestManager). Every objective/completion/failure flag is set by real walkable content (verified by sidequests.test.js), so these are LIVE tracked quests, not dead data. Design fields (region/giver/premise/theme/reward) are ignored by the engine and drive the quest log + bible.

sidequests.title: The Wake the World Withheld
sidequests.giver: Corliss, the sexton who marks the unmarked
sidequests.premise: A brisk woman in a mended black dress holds funerals for the souls no one ever mourned. Sit, if you'll help — there's a funeral starting, and the guest of honor has never once had anyone come.
sidequests.theme: To name the discarded is to defeat the Wall. A wake is an act of war: the grief is the guard.
sidequests.reward: act1.mercy_remembered; Corliss keeps the line, one small funeral at a time.

objectives.desc: Learn what Corliss does for the unmourned
Give Tatters the eulogy he waited sixty years for
Reach Brenn, who believes he deserves the silence

sidequests.title: The Last Lantern-Feast
sidequests.premise: A Baldur's Gate baker throws a forbidden party for the doomed, and the church is coming to put it out. Help her keep the lanterns lit.
sidequests.theme: Joy because it ends — the living refutation of the Deathless Garden.
sidequests.reward: Recruits the hidden eighth companion, Dot Marigold.

objectives.desc: Find the feast and meet Dot
Dance the last of it with Marisa
Thaw Sister Onora's frozen grief

sidequests.title: The Last Word
sidequests.premise: An unbeatable Returned of nine lifetimes holds court over a field he cleared without a scratch. Warn him before the loop takes him through the one small thing he won't watch.
sidequests.theme: The loop hunts the bright. You cannot save him — only change how he falls.
sidequests.reward: Warned/promised flags reshape the later set-piece of his death; his cracked grief can feed the Mournlight.

objectives.desc: Meet Sevrin and his little court
Make the unbeatable man promise to watch the small ones

sidequests.title: The After
sidequests.premise: The whistling has stopped. Return to the Last Word's lot to find who is left in the wreckage — and what your warnings bought.
sidequests.theme: You did everything right and he still died. The scar — and who survives it.
sidequests.reward: Sela can learn to carry her own fear; Tamsin can grieve honest; Coll is named the keeper of the dead's names.

objectives.desc: Steady Sela, who got her whole fear back at once
Take Tamsin down off the ceiling — death vs. unmaking

sidequests.title: The One Left
sidequests.region: Realm-side — the western corner of the lot
sidequests.premise: Past the last cairn, where the overlooked wash up: the souls a legend steps over. One of them is dissolving into the weapon that kills legends, and begging not to become a murder un-witnessed.
sidequests.theme: A soul who looks finds people where greatness found noise. The weapon is the victim.
sidequests.reward: Eases the Wall's truest victim; the read seeds the Lidless Eye's blank-page reproach.

objectives.desc: Witness Sift — the knife made of a person
Give Brynalla Coorne back her name
Stir the banked coal of the soul who chose to be nothing

sidequests.title: The Lidless Eye
sidequests.region: Behind your own eyes — off the Counting-House
sidequests.giver: the Unmade, the voice behind your eyes
sidequests.premise: A door with no other side. Here the three truths converge: the eye you were given was the enemy's, the cost of using it was becoming the monster, and the book you kept was the cure.
sidequests.theme: Witnessing as weapon and as ruin. You never had to tear the Wall down — only out-write it.
sidequests.reward: Reframes every dead-sense read; the full Niche-Book read aloud is the saga's true weapon.

objectives.desc: Face the narrator who was the Unmade all along
Reclaim the sight as your own
Read the full Niche-Book aloud — the refutation complete

sidequests.title: The Honest Devil
sidequests.region: Realm-side — the Long Odds, off the Night Market
sidequests.premise: The only warm, loud, well-lit room in the land of the dead — and the magnificent con-man who runs it has been waiting for a Returned to walk through his door.
sidequests.theme: The capitalist of grief. You cannot beat him with force — only out-deal, out-wager, name, or walk away clean.
sidequests.reward: A marker that opens a locked door later; the markers (owe / owed / walked) drive his recurrences.

objectives.desc: Strike a deal at his table
Cut the cards against the house
Leave money on the table — walk clean
Learn the one secret that could ruin him

sidequests.title: The Knife You Carry
sidequests.region: The Long Odds & the Wayward Mile
sidequests.premise: You hold the one thing that can destroy the Honest Devil — the truth that he secretly forgives debts for nothing. His whole house runs on the fear that a marker is iron. Decide.
sidequests.theme: The loaded gun. The villain you can't hate hands you his own throat.
sidequests.reward: Ruin him and free his hundred debtors — or protect the secret and keep a friend who knows the worst of him.

objectives.desc: Learn that Calloway laundered a mercy through you
Hand the Auditor the lever — ruin him, free the debtors
Keep the knife sheathed — protect his one decency

sidequests.title: What the Road Throws
sidequests.region: The Wayward Mile — a path that wasn't there a moment ago
sidequests.premise: A milestone at a bend you were sure was straight. Step close, and the road shows you a caprice — the kind of something that happens to a soul exactly once, and never the same something twice.
sidequests.theme: The strange visits a soul only so many times in one passage. Each one is unforgettable, and you'll only ever see a handful.
sidequests.reward: Keepsakes that ripple: the edge-coin, a name on a bone, a stranger's last word, an apocalypse in a case.

objectives.desc: Follow the Same Dog to the edge of the dissolving
Carry a stranger's last word out of the auction
Make the recursive beggar whole — with refusal
Free the comedian who died mid-joke
File a formal grievance against the Wall at the dead returns desk
Broker an end to the Two-Minute War that has lasted three centuries
Walk the Orchard of Hands and hold a letting-go you know
Return a stranger's mis-filed life, or share it
The Same Dog adopts you — name it, or let it anchor you against the cold

sidequests.title: The Intake Queue
sidequests.premise: An endless waiting-line for the newly dead, run by a clerk mortally afraid of being wrong. The Wall is the biggest clerk there is, hiding its cruelty in procedure so no one is ever to blame.
sidequests.theme: Bureaucracy as atrocity with no author; the souls who hide in the waiting.
sidequests.reward: Each soul in the queue seen past its procedure.

objectives.desc: See past the furious man's noise to his dread of the desk
See the kind one who made a home in the waiting

sidequests.title: A Custody of the Small
sidequests.giver: two souls fighting over a third
sidequests.premise: Two souls fight over the keeping of a child who is not theirs to keep. A custody battle in the land of the dead, where what's being fought over has its own small say.
sidequests.theme: Who does a soul belong to? The answer is: itself.
sidequests.reward: The child freed to choose.

sidequests.title: A Reunion Across an Age
sidequests.premise: Two souls separated by an age of death, a door, and the slow forgetting — and the third who kept the place between them. Some reunions take longer than a life.
sidequests.theme: Love that outlasts memory; the grief of arriving too late, or just in time.
sidequests.reward: A reunion completed; the keeper of the gap released.

objectives.desc: Witness Edran, who came the long way
Witness Mirren, who kept the place

sidequests.title: The Last Job
sidequests.giver: Greaves, who plans the impossible heist
sidequests.premise: A dead crew planning one last impossible job — to steal a soul back out of the nothing. The greatest heist there is, and the mark is the Wall itself.
sidequests.theme: Loyalty, the long con, and the one score you can't put down.
sidequests.reward: The crew witnessed; Rook freed; the score named for what it is.

objectives.desc: Free Rook from the debt that holds the crew
See Vesper, the one who never wanted the score

sidequests.title: The Long Truce
sidequests.premise: The war dead of a forgotten front, still holding their ground centuries on, because no one ever came to tell them it was over. A truce that only the living could call, called at last by the dead.
sidequests.theme: The wars that outlive their reasons; the courage it takes to be the first to lay down arms.
sidequests.reward: The line stood down; the soldiers witnessed on both sides.

objectives.desc: Witness Ander, who would not be the first to fall back
Witness Marek, on the other side of the same fear

sidequests.title: The Song That Remembers
sidequests.giver: Cale, who can't finish the song
sidequests.premise: A door of music, where a song half-remembered is the only thing keeping a soul from dissolving — and the only way to free it is to help it finish the verse.
sidequests.theme: Memory as anchor; the things we sing to keep the dead from sliding into the nothing.
sidequests.reward: The song finished; Cale freed; the harmony restored.

objectives.desc: See Pae, who carries the melody
See Wisp, who is fading into the rest

sidequests.title: The Unseen Hands
sidequests.giver: Wennick, who held the world up
sidequests.premise: The souls of the invisible labor — the ones who held the world up and were never once thanked or named. They built everything and are remembered by no one.
sidequests.theme: The dignity the world withholds from the hands that hold it up.
sidequests.reward: The unseen, finally seen and named.

objectives.desc: Witness Garrick, who carried more than his share
Witness Tilda, who kept the lamps lit for everyone else

sidequests.title: The Forbidden Loves
sidequests.giver: Mirae, who loved against the rule
sidequests.premise: Souls who loved where they were forbidden to, in life — and carry, in death, the question of whether a love the world condemned was a sin or the truest thing they ever did.
sidequests.theme: Love as defiance; the worth of a thing the world refused to allow.
sidequests.reward: The forbidden loves witnessed and affirmed; the condemnation answered.

objectives.desc: Witness Rell, who paid for it
Witness Wynn, who never regretted it

sidequests.title: A Refugee Named
sidequests.giver: a soul at the barred door
sidequests.premise: An almshouse of the dead under siege, and a refugee inside it the besiegers refuse to call by name — because a thing with no name is easier to turn away.
sidequests.theme: To name a refugee is to make them a person the door cannot refuse.
sidequests.reward: The refugee named, and made un-turn-away-able.

sidequests.title: The Ledger of Small Mercies
sidequests.premise: A journal that writes itself from your every kindness. You never see it being kept — but every soul you witness, every discarded name you bother to learn, is going down in a book that will matter more than any sword at the end.
sidequests.theme: The inverted deus ex machina: the kindness you spend is the only weapon that wins. Mercy, accumulated, is the cure.
sidequests.reward: The Niche-Book, full enough to out-write the Wall; the way back from the cold.

sidequests.title: The Second Death
sidequests.premise: A soul has been un-made — truly ended, not dissolved — which should be impossible. An Inquisitor wants a culprit. Investigate the one crime her order never wrote a punishment for, and deliver the verdict yourself.
sidequests.theme: The right to a true end. A soul trapped in the Wall's eternal torment-loop, a friend who loved her enough to open the door, and a law that lost the words 'against its will.' Is it murder, mercy, or the Wall's crime wearing the wrong name?
sidequests.reward: Your verdict: answer a murder by the statute, protect the friend with an honest lie, or argue the law itself overturned — and a soul out of eleven years of agony either freed or avenged.

objectives.desc: Hear the impossible case from Inquisitor Sahl
Learn from Liss what the Wall's torment-loop does to a soul
Learn that Edda begged for the door, every day, for eleven years
Learn whose hand opened it
